message:
chore(beads): sync latest concurrent issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1f42.8.5","title":"[QA-E2E] Complete scenario matrix for end-to-end integration scripts","description":"Task:
Produce and enforce an explicit E2E scenario completeness matrix covering success, failure, recovery, retry, interruption, and multi-provider parity paths.

Expected outputs:
- Matrix artifact mapping workflow -> script/test -> provider family -> expected evidence.
- Missing scenario scripts/tests added with deterministic setup and teardown.
- Explicit skip rationale for intentionally unsupported live paths.

Acceptance checks:
- Every matrix row is backed by a concrete executable test or documented waiver.
- run_all profile outputs reference matrix coverage in summary/evidence metadata.
- No high-risk workflow remains unowned/unmapped.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Canonical scenario matrix exists and each row maps to executable script/test or approved waiver.\n2. High-risk workflow classes (success, failure, recovery, retry, interruption) are represented with deterministic assertions.\n3. run_all artifacts reference matrix coverage and unresolved rows fail gating.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:42:57.301219288Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:34.454480611Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:45.378146026Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:42:57.301219288Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:45.092206390Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":980,"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"E2E anchor:
- tests/suite_classification.toml enumerates many e2e suites, but no single matrix currently proves complete workflow-to-scenario mapping with waiver accounting.

Dependency note:
Linked to bd-1f42.3.5 because soak/stability scenarios and logging depth are part of completion criteria.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:46:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1f42.8.5.1","title":"[QA-E2E] Generate canonical scenario-to-test coverage matrix artifact","description":"Build and version a canonical matrix mapping critical workflows to concrete E2E tests/scripts, provider families, and required artifacts. Acceptance: matrix is machine-readable, CI-consumed, and diffed for drift.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Canonical machine-readable matrix maps workflow -> script/test -> provider family -> expected artifacts.\n2. Matrix is versioned, CI-consumed, and drift-checked in gating.\n3. Every row has owner and explicit status (covered/waived/planned).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:44:33.317309217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:19.124067408Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:45.660464589Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:44:33.317309217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":992,"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Matrix should map: workflow -> suite/test -> provider family -> expected artifacts -> replay command. Store machine-readable artifact for CI diffing.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1f42.8.5.2","title":"[QA-E2E] Implement missing high-risk workflow scenarios","description":"For every uncovered high-risk row in the scenario matrix, add deterministic E2E scripts/tests with pass/fail assertions and artifact outputs. Acceptance: no high-risk workflow remains without executable coverage or approved waiver.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Uncovered high-risk rows receive deterministic executable scripts/tests with pass/fail assertions.\n2. Each new scenario emits required artifacts/logging per contract.\n3. No high-risk row remains unowned or undocumented.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:44:44.140403343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:27.564618955Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:44:44.140403343Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:45.946478894Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":993,"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implement scenarios for each uncovered high-risk matrix row; include positive and failure-path assertions with deterministic setup/teardown and artifact capture.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:03Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1f42.8.6","title":"[QA-E2E-LOG] Harden E2E logging contract and artifact quality gates","description":"Task:
Strengthen structured logging standards for E2E/unit integration runs so every failure yields deterministic, high-signal diagnostics.

Scope:
- Validate mandatory JSON/JSONL fields (correlation IDs, schema versions, timestamps, test IDs, replay hooks).
- Enforce per-suite test-log.jsonl + artifact-index.jsonl completeness and consistency.
- Tighten redaction, normalization, and cross-artifact linkage checks.

Acceptance checks:
- scripts/e2e/run_all.sh evidence_contract validation includes new strict checks where appropriate.
- Failure outputs include machine-parsable pointers to logs, artifacts, and replay commands.
- Logging schema/documentation versioning is updated and backward-compat waivers are explicit.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Logging schema/contract checks enforce required fields, linkage, redaction, and deterministic normalization.\n2. Every failed suite emits machine-readable digest + artifact pointers + replay metadata.\n3. CI fails on contract violations and prints targeted remediation guidance.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:43:03.671169456Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:40.670509469Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:46.819239731Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:43:03.671169456Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:10:39.894601808Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":981,"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Logging anchor:
- scripts/e2e/run_all.sh already emits environment.json, summary.json, per-suite result.json, test-log.jsonl, artifact-index.jsonl, and evidence_contract.json with strict validators.

Why this task still exists:
We need closure-quality guarantees on schema completeness, linkage integrity, and strict failure diagnostics for all targeted workflows.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:46:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1f42.8.6.1","title":"[QA-E2E-LOG] Enforce schema-required fields across JSON/JSONL artifacts","description":"Define and enforce required field sets for summary/result/test-log/artifact-index/evidence-contract outputs. Acceptance: validator fails on missing fields and emits targeted remediation guidance.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:01.334754026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:04.648306889Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:47.095221522Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:01.334754026Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":995,"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.6.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Schema enforcement should cover environment/summary/result/test-log/artifact-index/evidence-contract artifacts with strict required-key validation and clear failure messages.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1f42.8.6.2","title":"[QA-E2E-LOG] Ensure end-to-end correlation ID and artifact linkage integrity","description":"Verify that correlation_id and trace linkage are propagated consistently across environment.json, summary.json, result.json, test-log.jsonl, artifact-index.jsonl, and downstream readiness artifacts. Acceptance: cross-file linkage checks are strict in full-profile runs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:10.213541004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:04.987197270Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:47.381855111Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:10.213541004Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":996,"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.6.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correlation integrity means one run-level ID can be traced from top-level summary into per-suite logs/artifacts and downstream readiness/triage outputs.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:04Z"}]}

+{"id":"bd-3t3ct","title":"[PERF-5] Adaptive FPS under CPU pressure via /proc/loadavg monitoring","description":"## Problem

On an overloaded machine (many agents running, heavy compilation, etc.), the TUI should not waste CPU cycles rendering at 60fps when the user probably cannot even see the updates.

## Solution: CPU Pressure Detection + FPS Adaptation

### Detection
- Sample /proc/loadavg every 2 seconds (Linux) or host_processor_info (macOS)
- Compare 1-minute load average against available CPU count (from std::thread::available_parallelism())
- Pressure levels:
  - load < 1.0 × CPUs → Normal
  - load 1.0-2.0 × CPUs → Moderate pressure
  - load > 2.0 × CPUs → High pressure
  - load > 4.0 × CPUs → Critical pressure

### Adaptation
- Normal: 60fps (default)
- Moderate: 45fps
- High: 30fps
- Critical: 15fps

### Implementation

```rust
struct CpuPressureMonitor {
    cpu_count: usize,
    last_sample: Instant,
    sample_interval: Duration,  // 2 seconds
    current_load: f64,
    pressure_level: PressureLevel,
}
```

### Integration with Frame Budget (PERF-4)
- CPU pressure provides a FLOOR for the frame budget fidelity level
- If CPU pressure is Critical, fidelity cannot be higher than Degraded
- Frame budget can further reduce fidelity if individual frames exceed budget
- Combined: min(cpu_pressure_level, frame_budget_level)

### Platform Support
- Linux: /proc/loadavg (always available)
- macOS: sysctl("vm.loadavg") or libc::getloadavg()
- Windows/other: disable (always Normal)
- Feature-gated or runtime-detected

### Zero-Cost on Non-Linux
- Compile-time cfg for Linux-specific /proc reads
- On other platforms, CpuPressureMonitor always returns Normal

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs: CpuPressureMonitor struct, integration with FrameBudget

## Dependencies
- Depends on PERF-4 (frame budget — this layer feeds into budget decisions)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CpuPressureMonitor reads /proc/loadavg on Linux
- [ ] Pressure levels correctly mapped from load/cpu_count ratio
- [ ] FPS adapted based on pressure level
- [ ] Integration with frame budget state machine
- [ ] Unit tests for pressure level calculation
- [ ] Unit tests for combined pressure + budget decision
- [ ] Graceful no-op on non-Linux platforms","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:31.312915658Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:31.312915658Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3tc8","title":"Fix malformed string literals in tests/reproduction_truncate.rs","description":"cargo check --all-targets currently fails with E0762 (unterminated character literal) in tests/reproduction_truncate.rs due broken multiline string/comment quoting. Normalize literals/expectations to valid escaped Rust strings so test target compiles.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:40:01.114488845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T01:45:37.042422542Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T01:45:37.042396724Z","close_reason":"Completed: reproduction_truncate compile blocker no longer present; all-target gate passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
